a11y.chart.cashflow.label: Stacked cash flow chart by year with a cumulative Net CF line on the secondary axis
a11y.chart.ebitda.label: Stacked EBITDA chart for 2026–2028 with a margin line on the secondary axis
a11y.chart.irr.label: IRR sensitivity heatmap 7×4: 7 discount rates on the vertical axis, 4 horizons on the horizontal axis, color encodes the return zone, active cell highlighted
a11y.chart.mc.label: Monte Carlo IRR distribution histogram: 30 bars spanning -10% to 35% with P5, P50, P95 markers and the deterministic IRR line at 20.09%
a11y.chart.peers.label: Peer scatter chart: EV/EBITDA vs IRR historic. Six synthetic peer points and one gold star for the TrendStudio project. Use Tab to move between points, Enter to drill down.
a11y.chart.pipeline.label: Pipeline Gantt chart: 7 projects across the 2025-Q1 to 2028-Q4 horizon, colour-coded by stage with release markers
a11y.chart.revenue.label: Revenue waterfall 2026–2028 by scenario
a11y.control.horizon.label: Slider: investment horizon in years from 3 to 10
a11y.control.rate.label: Slider: discount rate in percent from 10 to 25
a11y.control.stress.label: Slider: stress test in percent from 0 to 100
a11y.drilldown.cashflow.label: Cash flow detail dialog for the selected year with methodology and figures
a11y.drilldown.ebitda.label: EBITDA breakdown detail dialog with methodology and per-year figures
a11y.drilldown.irr.label: IRR Sensitivity detail dialog with WACC anchor methodology, sensitivity formula and risk-zone thresholds
a11y.drilldown.mc.label: Monte Carlo IRR bin detail dialog with simulation methodology and stress-scenario parameters
a11y.drilldown.peers.label: Peer comparison details: name, country, multiples, and synthetic-data flag.
a11y.drilldown.pipeline.label: Pipeline project detail dialog with code, genre, stage, timeline and financial parameters
a11y.drilldown.revenue.label: Revenue year detail

ui.chart.cashflow.title: Cash Flow by Year
ui.chart.ebitda.title: EBITDA and Margin by Year
ui.chart.irr.title: IRR Sensitivity — 7×4 matrix
ui.chart.mc.title: Monte Carlo — IRR Distribution
ui.chart.peers.title: Peer set: EV/EBITDA × IRR (synthetic)
ui.chart.pipeline.title: Project Pipeline 2025–2028
ui.chart.revenue.title: Revenue by year
ui.control.horizon.label: Horizon, years
ui.control.rate.label: Discount rate, %
ui.control.scenario.label: Scenario
ui.control.stress.label: Stress test, %
ui.drilldown.cashflow.explanation: Annual cash flow consists of three components: operating (ongoing business), investing (capex into content production) and financing (capital raised, debt service). Net CF = Op + Inv + Fin. The cumulative line plots running Net CF at year end: the portfolio turns positive after 2030 releases and finishes the horizon at cumulative approx 1,250 RUB million.
ui.drilldown.cashflow.methodology: Source: cashflow.yearly (deck_data v1.2.0) with five anchor years: 2026 (investing), 2029 (financing), 2030 (first operating inflows plus remaining financing), 2031-2032 (pure operating inflows post-release). Scenarios: bull scales operating and financing by x1.2, bear by x0.6; investing is held flat (capex committed). Cumulative is the running sum of Net CF across years from 2026 to 2032.
ui.drilldown.cashflow.title: Cash flow — per-year detail
ui.drilldown.common.title: Details
ui.drilldown.common.warning_synthetic: ⚠ Synthetic data: peer-set will be updated in Phase 2C after NDA
ui.drilldown.ebitda.explanation: EBITDA equals revenue minus direct costs (COGS) and operating expenses (OPEX). Margin is the ratio of EBITDA to revenue in percent. Under the bull scenario EBITDA is scaled by x1.25, under bear by x0.60.
ui.drilldown.ebitda.methodology: Source: pnl.ebitda_breakdown (deck_data v1.2.0) across three years 2026–2028. COGS and OPEX are expressed as negative values so Revenue + COGS + OPEX = EBITDA. Average 3-year margin in the base case is 47.7%. Bull/Bear apply stress multipliers to EBITDA while holding revenue and costs flat, so the margin moves proportionally with EBITDA.
ui.drilldown.ebitda.title: EBITDA breakdown — detail view
ui.drilldown.irr.explanation: The heatmap shows how the portfolio IRR responds to two key inputs: the discount rate (10–25%) and the investment horizon (3–10 years). Cell color encodes the risk zone: red (IRR < 10%) is below WACC, yellow (10–18%) is a moderate premium, green (> 18%) is the target return, dark-green (> 25%) is a premium scenario. The active cell is outlined in gold and tracks the current slider position.
ui.drilldown.irr.methodology: Anchor: WACC = 19%, horizon = 5 years, deterministic IRR = 20.09% (anchor in deck_data.sensitivity.irr_matrix). Sensitivity formula: IRR(r, h) = IRR_det × (1 − α·(r − r₀)/r₀ + β·(h − h₀)/h₀), with α ≈ 0.28 (rate elasticity), β ≈ 0.14 (horizon elasticity), r₀ = 19%, h₀ = 5 years. The matrix is monotone: IRR falls as the rate rises and grows as the horizon extends. All 7×4 = 28 cells are pre-computed and validated in the S41 audit.
ui.drilldown.irr.title: IRR Sensitivity — detail view
ui.drilldown.mc.explanation: The histogram shows the IRR distribution across 50,000 Monte Carlo simulations. The gold dashed line at 20.09% marks the deterministic target IRR (primary headline metric). Coloured markers indicate percentiles P5 (-0.4%), P50 (12.0%) and P95 (21.1%). The stress slider shifts the mean and widens the dispersion according to the stress_levels table.
ui.drilldown.mc.methodology: Source: mc.irr_distribution (30 bins, 1.5 pp wide), mc.percentiles, mc.stress_levels. N = 50,000 iterations, seed = 42, 5 stochastic variables: release timing (Poisson lambda=0.5), hit rate (Triangular 0.40/0.70/1.00), budget variance, discount factor, FX risk. For stress > 0 the bins are re-sampled analytically as N(mu + mean_shift, sigma * sigma_mult) with linear interpolation between the 0/25/50/75/100 anchor levels.
ui.drilldown.mc.title: Monte Carlo — bin detail
ui.drilldown.peers.explanation: All values are synthetic-mock; a real peer set will arrive in Phase 2C after NDA access. Comparison is illustrative of positioning only.
ui.drilldown.peers.methodology: X-axis: EV/EBITDA (×) — enterprise value to EBITDA for the latest reporting period. Y-axis: IRR historic (%) — historical return on capital on the studio's 3–5 year project portfolio. Bubble size is proportional to EV/Revenue. Color encodes country (RU/US/FR). The gold star is the TrendStudio project with IRR-projected under the base scenario. Multiple validation and NDA access to trading comps — Phase 2C (v1.2.1).
ui.drilldown.peers.title: Peer comparison: details
ui.drilldown.pipeline.explanation: Project card: code, genre/type (film or series), production stage (Script → Development → Pre-Production → Production → Post-Production → Release), quarterly production horizon, release date, budget and projected revenue in RUB million. The Gantt bar colour reflects the current stage; diagonal stripes mark a series (vs. a solid bar for a film); a dashed line with a gear icon marks the release date.
ui.drilldown.pipeline.methodology: Source: pipeline.projects (deck_data v1.2.0). Horizon 2025-Q1 … 2028-Q4 (16 quarters). Budget covers direct producer costs; revenue is the consolidated sales plan across all windows (theatrical + digital + TV). Bull/Bear scenarios scale revenue via the pipeline.revenue_by_year multipliers; production timelines and budgets stay constant.
ui.drilldown.pipeline.name: Title
ui.drilldown.pipeline.title: Pipeline project — detail view
ui.drilldown.revenue.explanation: Waterfall shows each year's contribution to the 3Y total 4,545 M RUB (base).
ui.drilldown.revenue.methodology: Base — consensus plan. Bull +20%, Bear −30% — stresses from channel-mix (box office volatility, SVOD pricing).
ui.drilldown.revenue.title: Revenue — year drill-down

ui.project.p2.name: The Northern Route
ui.project.p3.genre: Melodramatic series — a Moscow family across the turn of an era, three generations in a single apartment
ui.project.p3.name: A House on Arbat
ui.project.p4.genre: Action-disaster film — a firefighter and a rescuer on an emergency mission in the mountains
ui.project.p4.name: Fire and Water
ui.project.p5.genre: Saga series — a fight over the family business, intrigue and generational conflict
ui.project.p5.name: The Heirs
ui.project.p6.genre: Musical — musicians of a provincial conservatoire in search of a bigger stage
ui.project.p6.name: Song of the Horizon
ui.project.p7.genre: Biographical drama — a portrait of a Soviet-era explorer in his mature years
